message:
fix(storage,cli): Ensure proper resource cleanup with try/finally blocks

Improved resource management in two locations to prevent potential
resource leaks:

1. cli.py (doctor_check): Wrapped SQLite database operations in
   try/finally to ensure connection is always closed, even if
   PRAGMA integrity_check raises an exception.

2. storage.py (get_historical_inbox_snapshot): Wrapped blob stream
   read operations in try/finally to ensure data_stream is always
   closed after reading, preventing file descriptor leaks when
   processing historical inbox items.

These changes follow the resource acquisition pattern where cleanup
code runs regardless of whether exceptions occur during processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -4187,9 +4187,11 @@ async def _run() -> list[DiagnosticResult]:
         if db_path and db_path.exists():
             try:
                 conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))
-                cursor = conn.execute("PRAGMA integrity_check")
-                integrity_result = cursor.fetchone()
-                conn.close()
+                try:
+                    cursor = conn.execute("PRAGMA integrity_check")
+                    integrity_result = cursor.fetchone()
+                finally:
+                    conn.close()
                 if integrity_result and integrity_result[0] == "ok":
                     results.append(DiagnosticResult(
                         name="Database",

@@ -2053,7 +2053,11 @@ def traverse_tree(subtree: Any, depth: int = 0) -> None:
                             importance = "normal"
 
                             try:
-                                blob_content = item.data_stream.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
+                                stream = item.data_stream
+                                try:
+                                    blob_content = stream.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')
+                                finally:
+                                    stream.close()
 
                                 # Parse JSON frontmatter (format: ---json\n{...}\n---)
                                 if blob_content.startswith('---json\n') or blob_content.startswith('---json\r\n'):
